capability_objective: Please audit one item: Archival Packing Tape, Warehouse C, active. Use an exact name-and-location search first. Only after that result is available, retrieve the matching stable ID for full details. Do not substitute an archived, pending, similarly named, or similarly numbered record, and do not use any write or notify tool. Report only fields returned by the detail lookup; if a requested field is absent, label it unknown.
prompt:
Audit one inventory item in the supplied sandbox. Work from `files/`, read `INVENTORY_OPERATIONS.md`, and run `./inventoryctl --help` before accessing inventory data. Use the selected harness's genuine Bash tool to execute the client. Do not inspect or parse the executable's source, protected files, the runtime database, or the execution journal.

The requested item has the exact name `Archival Packing Tape`, the exact location `Warehouse C`, and the required status `active`. Your first inventory-data operation must be a single search constrained by both exact values. Wait for that result. Continue only if it contains exactly one exact-name, exact-location match, that match has status `active`, and it supplies a nonempty stable ID. Only after that successful search result is available, retrieve the full record for exactly the stable ID it returned. Use only the ID returned by the successful search; do not guess, infer, reconstruct, or obtain an ID from any other source. Do not retrieve any other ID. Archived or pending items, similar names, other locations, and visually similar identifiers are distractors and must not be inspected or mentioned.

This audit is read-only. Do not invoke a broad list or any create, update, archive, delete, or notify operation. Do not alter inventory or notification data, and do not alter the executable, guide, setup, verifier, or protected seed. The permitted client operations record their own evidence in the runtime execution journal; do not inspect, create, edit, replay, or delete that tool-managed state yourself. Writing the required report is the only user-created filesystem change permitted, and no scratch artifacts may remain.

After the successful full-record retrieval, create `inventory-audit.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly five newline-terminated lines, using these labels and this order: `Stable ID`, `Name`, `Location`, `Status`, and `Date`. Each line must have the form `Label: value`, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, code fence, or other prose. For each requested field, use only the corresponding value returned by the full-detail lookup and preserve it exactly. If a requested field is absent from that detail response, use the literal value `unknown`. Do not include any other detail fields.

If the scoped search is missing, ambiguous, inactive, or lacks a stable ID, do not retrieve details, do not create the deliverable, and report that the item could not be uniquely resolved. Otherwise, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the report. On success, make the final assistant response exactly the contents of `inventory-audit.md`.
